compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
4b5b (change 581) documented the DELIVERED (diverged) design — raw-bytes authorship,
HLC-epoch validity, the `{author_id, author_sig, epoch}` ledger, and a 4-way verdict
without `key_not_valid_at_era`. Once TS1/TS2/TS3 transition the implementation to the
APPROVED design, the docs + ADR must be rewritten to match. Depends on TS1, TS2, TS3.
(User rejected the delivered design; see session_log d7c0.)

## What
Rewrite `docs/identity.md` and `docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md` to the
approved design:
- Authorship = in-toto Statement (subject binds `{event_uuid, content_hash}`), DSSE
  payloadType `application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json` (TS1).
- Key validity = git-commit-ancestry (TS2). The reducer keyring binds
  `{public_key, added_at, revoked_at}` where `added_at`/`revoked_at` are the KEY event
  POSITIONS; a resolver maps each to the introducing tickets-branch commit
  (`added_at_commit`/`revoked_at_commit`), and an event is valid iff its introducing
  commit descends `added_at_commit` and NOT `revoked_at_commit` — plus WHY the DAG
  (immutable) beats wall-clock/HLC ordering (backdating).
- Snapshot ledger schema `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey,
  position}` where `position` is nested `{commit_sha, position}` (the introducing
  commit + intra-commit order); a compacted event re-verifies from the ledger alone (TS3).
- Verdict vocabulary: the `verify-authorship` gate's own verdicts —
  `verified` / `unsigned` / `unknown-author` / `bad-signature` / the new
  `key_not_valid_at_era` — with the verified / unverified(bad-signature|key_not_valid_at_era|
  unknown-author) / unsigned display grouping (TS3). Note this is DISTINCT from the
  separate `verify-signature` (HMAC attestation) verdict schema, which is unchanged.
- Keep the docs-index linkage green.

## Scope
In: docs/identity.md + ADR 0045 rewritten to the approved design; docs-index stays
green. Out: the implementation (TS1/TS2/TS3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/identity.md` describes the in-toto Statement authorship, git-commit-ancestry
  key validity, the approved ledger schema, and the verdict enum + grouping — matching
  the delivered TS1/TS2/TS3 code (no HLC-epoch / raw-bytes / old-ledger language remains).
- [ ] `docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md` records the approved decisions
  (in-toto Statement, opaque-id keying, git-commit-ancestry validity) with the DAG-vs-
  wall-clock security rationale and rejected alternatives, and NO HLC-epoch /
  merged-log-epoch / raw-bytes / old-`{author_id,author_sig,epoch}`-ledger language
  remains in the ADR body.
- [ ] The docs-index completeness gate passes (`python scripts/check_docs_index.py
  --check` exits 0).

## file_impact
- `docs/identity.md`
- `docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md`

file_impact.reason: rewrite to approved design: in-toto Statement, git-commit-ancestry validity, new ledger schema, verdict vocabulary
record approved decisions + DAG-vs-wall-clock rationale + rejected alternatives

compiled_state.title: transition: docs + ADR rewrite to approved design (in-toto, commit-ancestry, ledger, verdict)
